2006-10-01.title: Suicide Bomber Kills 12 Near Kabul Ministry
2006-10-01.abstract: The second major suicide attack this month underscored the rising danger in the Afghan capital.

2006-10-01.title: Scandal Shadows Brazil's Election
2006-10-01.abstract: Political analysts are uncertain if Brazil’s president, made vulnerable by another corruption scandal, will obtain the majority he needs to avoid a runoff.

2006-10-01.abstract: Hungary’s president made the request on national television as the polls closed in local government elections.

2006-10-01.abstract: Documents show a 92-year-old former police officer was found guilty of taking part in the Novi Sad massacre in Serbia, ending his 60 years of anonymity.

2006-10-01.abstract: Experts agree that demand must be contained, particularly in agriculture, and supply increased.

2006-10-02.abstract: A plan to slice Shimokitazawa in half with an 81-foot-wide road has set off a rare battle for preservation in one of Asia’s trendiest metropolises.

2006-10-02.abstract: Six Palestinians were killed in clashes between Hamas and Fatah in some of their fiercest battles since the elections in January.

2006-10-02.abstract: The fugitives, who were sentenced to death for a French oil tanker attack, escaped from a high security prison six months ago.

2006-10-02.abstract: The foreign minister of South Korea won overwhelming support in a final informal poll of the Security Council.

2006-10-03.abstract: Palestinian leaders appealed for calm, and the clashes were not as intense or as widespread as on Sunday.

2006-10-03.abstract: The foreign minister of South Korea is virtually assured of being selected as the next secretary general of the United Nations.

2006-10-03.abstract: Bangalore, where dozens of multinational companies have operations, will completely shut down on Wednesday as part of a protest over a border issue.

2006-10-04.abstract: While the pilots have not been charged, the order was issued as a “preventive measure.”

2006-10-04.title: In Graft Inquiry, Chinese See a Shake-Up Coming
2006-10-04.abstract: Analysts say China’s rulers are trying to shake up the leadership and wipe out resistance to their policy agenda.

2006-10-04.title: Japan Leader’s First Diplomatic Bow Is to Asian Neighbors
2006-10-04.abstract: The newly installed administration of Prime Minister Shinzo Abe is moving rapidly to improve estranged relations with China and South Korea.

2006-10-04.title: Ireland: Premier Apologizes
2006-10-04.abstract: Prime Minister Bertie Ahern, trying to end the biggest crisis of his nine years in power, apologized for accepting money from friends in the early 1990’s, which he has characterized as loans. His partners in the coalition government said they would continue to support him. “It was a misjudgment, although not in breach of any law or code of conduct,” he told lawmakers in the Parliament.

2006-10-04.title: Key Neighbors Try to Dissuade North Korea
2006-10-04.abstract: South Korea and China warned North Korea that a nuclear test would lead to a chill in relations.

2006-10-05.title: Russia: Bill Would Move Gambling to 4 Zones, Including Siberia
2006-10-05.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin called for sweeping restrictions on casinos and gambling halls, which have proliferated across Moscow and other cities in Russia in recent years. Meeting with party leaders from Parliament, Mr. Putin, left, described gambling as an affliction comparable to “the spread of alcoholism among the population.” The speaker of the upper house, Boris V. Gryzlov, later said that the president’s proposal would restrict all gambling to four unpopulated regions: two in European Russia, one in Siberia and one in the Far East. He provided few other details of the legislation, but the changes, if enacted, would affect a burgeoning industry worth billions of dollars.

2006-10-05.title: German Leader Survives With Health Care Deal
2006-10-05.abstract: Chancellor Angela Merkel reached an agreement for new financing of Germany’s health care system.

2006-10-05.title: Turkish Hijacker Acted Alone, Officials Say, Rejecting a Larger Plot
2006-10-05.abstract: Italian and Turkish authorities agreed on Wednesday that Hakan Ekinci acted alone in Tuesday’s hijacking of a Turkish airliner.

2006-10-05.title: Commission Says I.R.A. Has Halted Terror Activity
2006-10-05.abstract: The positive report potentially removes a major obstacle to the restoration of Northern Ireland’s legislature, which was suspended in 2003.

2006-10-05.title: U.S. Plan Would Expand Palestinian Leader’s Security Force
2006-10-05.abstract: The expansion would be part of a $26 million plan to shore up the Palestinian leader’s position, according to donors.

2006-10-06.title: Cluster Bombs
2006-10-06.abstract: In southern Lebanon, cluster bombs are the most serious threat facing the  civilian population. (Producers: Micah Garen and Marie-Helene Carleton)

2006-10-06.title: Women Face Greatest Threat of Violence at Home, Study Finds

2006-10-07.title: Hungary: Prime Minister Holds On
2006-10-07.abstract: Prime Minister Ferenc Gyurcsany won a vote of confidence in Parliament by a vote of 207 to 165, even as thousands of protesters gathered to rally against him outside the huge building in Budapest. Mr. Gyurcsany has been under fire since last month, when the Hungarian news media broadcast an audiotape of him admitting that his party had lied to the public about the health of the economy. Mr. Gyurcsany had called for the vote of confidence to reinforce his mandate and to strengthen support for his program of economic austerity.

2006-10-07.title: Russia Deports Georgians and Increases Pressures on Businesses and Students
2006-10-07.abstract: Russia deported 153 citizens of Georgia as a wave of punitive measures against Georgian migrants and businesses grew.

2006-10-07.title: Friedrich Karl Flick, 79, Heir to a German Industrial Empire, Dies
2006-10-07.abstract: Mr. Flick was an heir to vast wealth, a father’s notoriety and a name that stood for a sinister nexus of politics and big business.

2006-10-07.abstract: American diplomats conceded that there could still be difficult negotiations over what penalties to impose and their timing.

He held separate talks with the Palestinian Authority president, Mahmoud Abbas, of Fatah, and Prime Minister Ismail Haniya, of Hamas. Sheik Hamad presented a six-point plan for the unity government, but Hamas rejected two of them, the recognition of Israel and the renunciation of violence, according to Palestinian officials.

2006-10-11.abstract: The sectarian violence in the capital showed little sign of easing, despite efforts by the American military and its Iraqi counterparts to quell it.

2006-10-11.abstract: The project, which is intended to supply computers broadly to children in developing nations, was conceived by a computer researcher at M.I.T.

2006-10-12.abstract: In Argentina, Gen. Juan Domingo Perón’s ideological heirs want his body moved to a mausoleum, but a woman who claims to be his daughter wants a DNA test conducted first.

One NATO soldier was among the dead and another was wounded. The governor of Kandahar, Asadullah Khaled, said the soldiers involved were Americans serving as part of the NATO force in the south. More than 200 people have been killed in Afghanistan in suicide attacks this year.

2006-10-20.abstract: Ramadan is a rare occasion when many Muslims are permitted to worship at one of Islam’s holiest sites.

2006-10-20.title: Britain: Veiled Muslim Loses Discrimination Case
2006-10-20.abstract: An employment tribunal found that Aishah Azmi, the Muslim classroom assistant suspended from her job for wearing a full-face veil, had not been discriminated against or harassed on religious grounds. But she was awarded $1,850 in damages for “injury to feelings.” Her case has been a part of a furious debate in Britain about the wearing of the niqab, the veil that covers all but a woman’s eyes. After the hearing in northern England, Ms. Azmi, 24, criticized Prime Minister Tony Blair and other government ministers for commenting on her case while it was under way.

2006-10-20.abstract: South Korea told the secretary of state that it would not end the ventures, even though they help fund the North Korean government.

2006-10-21.abstract: With investment from Asia and the Middle East, the Sudanese capital Khartoum has  transformed into a burgeoning African center. (Producers: Adam B. Ellick and Courtenay Morris)

2006-10-22.abstract: Princess Irene Galitzine was a descendant of Russian nobility who founded a glamorous fashion house in Italy.

2006-10-28.abstract: United Nations forces rescued a politician who had been kidnapped in an increasingly edgy prelude to the runoff presidential election.

2006-10-28.title: U.N. to Limit the Role of Its Envoy Expelled by Sudan
2006-10-28.abstract: Jan Pronk will continue to hold his office until his contract expires at the end of the year, but his role is expected to be mainly symbolic.

2006-10-28.title: Chile: Pinochet’s Arrest Ordered
2006-10-28.abstract: An investigative judge ordered Gen. Augusto Pinochet arrested for cases of kidnapping, torture and murder that occurred at the Villa Grimaldi secret detention center outside Santiago in the early years of his dictatorship. General Pinochet, 90, who ruled from 1973 to 1990, was questioned about the crimes earlier this month and said that he was not responsible for the human rights violations, and also that he could not remember what had happened at the notorious site. Chile’s current president, Michelle Bachelet, and her mother, Ángela Jeria, were among the political prisoners held and tortured at Villa Grimaldi, but they are not plaintiffs in the case.

2006-10-28.title: Mexico: American Among Dead in Oaxaca
2006-10-28.abstract: A New York journalist and three other people were shot and killed during protests in the southern city of Oaxaca as left-wing groups seeking to oust Gov. Ulises Ruiz tried to strangle the city with blockades and encountered resistance from the police and ordinary citizens.

2006-10-29.title: Boomtown in Venezuela Confronts Mining Ban
2006-10-29.abstract: President Hugo Chavez has halted mining in a Venezuelan town, setting off protests and threatening the livelihoods of miners.

2006-10-29.title: Mexican Federal Police, Backed by Army, Retake City
2006-10-29.abstract: Federal forces bashed through barricades and pushed back activists who had seized downtown Oaxaca five months ago.

2006-10-29.title: Seeking to Ease Rift, Bush Confers With Iraq Premier
2006-10-29.abstract: President Bush held a videoconference with Baghdad after days of angry comments by Iraqi leaders about what they see as U.S. meddling.

2006-10-29.title: Chirac’s Last Act: Lion in Winter, Wolves All Around
2006-10-29.abstract: French President Jacques Chirac has been under unrelenting attack at home, as old allies criticize his leadership and question his legacy.

2006-10-29.title: Missing Soldier Said to Be Wed to an Iraqi
2006-10-29.abstract: The secret marriage, if confirmed, would not only be highly unusual but also a violation of American military rules.

2006-10-30.title: Spate of Bombs in Baghdad Kills 46 Iraqis
2006-10-30.abstract: The American toll for October rose to 101, the highest since January 2005, with the military’s announcement of two additional deaths.

2006-10-30.title: U.S. Is Said to Fail in Tracking Arms Shipped to Iraqis
2006-10-30.abstract: Hundreds of thousands of weapons intended for Iraqi security forces were not properly monitored, a new federal report said.

2006-10-30.title: Attackers Set Fire to Bus in Marseille, Wounding One
2006-10-30.abstract: Attackers set public bus on fire in Marseille, France, critically wounding young woman; bus attack is eighth in last week, but first in which anyone is harmed; attacks began during days before one-year anniversary of death of two teenagers in Paris suburb that had inflamed feelings of resentment in France's poor immigrant communities and sparked three-week wave of unrest (S)

2006-10-30.title: Yemen Arrests 8 It Links to Plot by Al Qaeda
2006-10-30.abstract: Security officials in Yemen arrest eight men, including several Europeans, Australians and a Somali, in what is described as plot linked to Al Qaeda to smuggle weapons to Somalia from Yemen; if men are convicted, it may offer most concrete link of growing Qaeda presence in Somalia (S)

2006-10-30.title: Mexican Forces Move to Retake Oaxaca
2006-10-30.abstract: Federal police armed with water cannons and backed by heavily armed soldiers advance on protesters in Oaxaca, Mex, who have occupied main square of city during five-month standoff; operation, ordered by Pres Vicente Fox in his final month in office, does not become bloodbath that many had feared; protesters are seeking removal of governor of Oaxaca State, Ulises Ruiz, who sent in poorly trained riot police officers to oust striking teachers in June, and who has been accused of rigging 2004 gubernatorial election and ruling state with iron hand; photo; map (M)

2006-10-31.title: Olmert Says Israel May Widen Military Role in Gaza
2006-10-31.abstract: Israel’s prime minister said the military operation would be intended to halt Palestinian rocket fire, not to reoccupy the territory.

2006-10-31.title: U.S.-Led Exercise in Persian Gulf Sets Sights on Deadliest Weapons
2006-10-31.abstract: us-led exercise in persian gulf sets sights on deadliest weapons**More than two dozen countries, including three gulf states, practice intercepting and searching vessels suspected of trafficking in unconventional weapons in major military maneuvers that emphasize their coordination and willingness to aggressively block spread of arms; exercise, 20 miles outside Iranian territorial waters, seen as signal to Iran that coalition of Western powers and neighboring states is intent on denying it access to nuclear, chemical and biological weapons, even on its doorstep; exercise is notable for involvement of Bahrain, and support by Qatar and United Arab Emirates; Saudi Arabia, power in Persian Gulf, is absent; Iran warns exercise's participants against acts that could destabilize region; initiative was first proposed by Pres Bush in 2003 (M)

2006-10-31.title: Mexican Protesters Keep Their Message Alive, and on the Air
2006-10-31.abstract: As federal riot police hunkered down in Oaxaca, protesters sought to protect the pilfered radio transmitter they use to mobilize the population.

2006-10-31.title: World Briefing | Americas: Canada: U.S. Sends Reply, But No Apology, In Case Of Deported Man
2006-10-31.abstract: Sec of State Condoleezza Rice sends assurance to Canadian officials that they will be consulted in future about any Canadians who may be sent to third country against their will; pledge is in response to official protest over treatment of Maher Arar, Canadian deported to Syria from New York based on inaccurate terrorism allegations (S)

2006-10-31.title: U.S. Envoy Arrives in Iraq as Tough Options Loom
2006-10-31.abstract: Pres Bush's national security adviser Stephen J Hadley makes unannounced trip to Baghdad to discuss how to pull country back from brink; options said to include substantial increase in number of American and Iraqi troops patrolling Baghdad; other proposals now being discussed range from how to permit greater autonomy for Shiite, Sunni and Kurdish sections of country without splitting country apart; how to share oil revenues among Iraq's population; and amnesty for those who attacked Iraqi or American troops; before his trip, Hadley described series of American goals, including new effort to get more financial and other support from neighboring states; critics of Bush administration, many of them Republican, say administration must lower its sights for democratic Iraq and simply regain semblance of stability there; photo (M)
